steps.title: Find the plural subject
steps.content: The subject is "lost works," a plural noun phrase. The example of Lucan's poem Laudes Neronis sits inside that phrase, and the dash phrase "no copy of which exists" is parenthetical. The verb must agree with the plural subject, not the singular example.

steps.title: Reject singular agreement
steps.content: B uses the singular form "is becoming." C uses the singular form "has become." D uses the singular present-tense form "becomes." Since the subject is plural "works," the verb must be plural. The answer is A.
